talents.name: Opportunist
talents.summary: Once per round, after rolling a plot die, you may reroll it; the new result stands.

talents.name: Watchful Eye
talents.summary: When a willing ally within 20 feet whom you can influence rolls a plot die, apply your Opportunist reroll to their die as if it were yours.

talents.name: Get 'Em Talking
talents.summary: Spend 1 focus; test Deduction vs. Spiritual defense of a character you can influence. Success reveals their motivation for this scene, and for the rest of the scene you may raise the stakes on tests that leverage it.

talents.name: Quick Analysis
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus to gain 2 actions usable only for cognitive skill tests via Use a Skill, Gain Advantage, or Agent-talent actions. In conversations/endeavors, the GM may let you convert both into an extra contribution.

talents.name: Baleful
talents.summary: Characters must spend extra focus equal to your tier to resist your influence.

talents.name: Gather Evidence
talents.summary: After succeeding on a cognitive test against a character, become Focused until the end of your next turn. On acquisition: gain the Legal Codes utility expertise.

talents.name: Hardy
talents.summary: Maximum and current health increase by 1 per level, retroactively and for all future levels.

talents.name: Sleuth's Instincts
talents.summary: Advantage on cognitive tests against characters whose motivation you know, and you automatically detect (but not decode) their lies to you.

talents.name: Close the Case
talents.summary: Once per scene, spend 3 focus; test Deduction vs. Cognitive defense of a character you can influence (advantage if you know their motivation, disadvantage if not). Success: they back down for the scene (resisting costs them a disadvantage until end of next turn). Failure: they gain advantage on tests against you until end of their next turn.

talents.name: Sure Outcome
talents.summary: When using Opportunist, instead of rerolling you may spend 2 focus to flip the plot die: an Opportunity face flips to Complication [+4]; any Complication face flips to Opportunity.

talents.name: Plausible Excuse
talents.summary: After being discovered skulking, spend 2 focus to influence everyone sensing you to accept an innocent excuse; resisting costs extra focus equal to your Deception ranks. On acquisition: gain the Sleight of Hand utility expertise.

talents.name: Collected
talents.summary: Cognitive and Spiritual defenses increase by 2.

talents.name: Cover Story
talents.summary: Establish a false identity (details agreed with the GM) and gain a cultural expertise fitting that identity.

talents.name: Subtle Takedown
talents.summary: Unarmed attack using Insight vs. Cognitive defense of a target (your size or smaller) that is Surprised, can't sense you, or doesn't see you as a threat; raise the stakes. On a hit they can't communicate until end of their next turn, and you may spend an Opportunity to auto-succeed a 1-action Grapple that also leaves them Prone while Restrained.

talents.name: Mighty
talents.summary: On a weapon or unarmed hit, each action spent on the attack adds 1 + your tier extra damage.

talents.name: Mercurial Facade
talents.summary: Disguise yourself with Deception instead of Thievery, no supplies needed. The first character to see through the disguise (or be deliberately shown it) is Surprised until end of your next turn.

talents.name: High Society Contacts
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus to add an Opportunity to a test made to interact socially in high society. On acquisition: gain the High Society cultural expertise.

talents.name: Risky Behavior
talents.summary: Before making a test, spend 1 focus to raise the stakes on it yourself.

talents.name: Cheap Shot
talents.summary: With a free hand, spend 1 focus: unarmed attack using Thievery vs. Cognitive defense of an enemy your size or smaller, raising the stakes. On a hit: Stunned until end of their next turn. On a hit or graze: spend an Opportunity to steal a carried (not held) item, GM's discretion.

talents.name: Double Down
talents.summary: After using Opportunist, reroll the plot die one more time; you must keep the final result, and if it shows a Complication you lose 2 focus.

talents.name: Surefooted
talents.summary: Movement rate increases by 10 feet, and damage from falling or dangerous terrain is reduced by 2 × your tier.

talents.name: Underworld Contacts
talents.prerequisiteOther: Patron or follower in the criminal underworld
talents.summary: Before a test to interact socially with criminals, spend 2 focus to add an Opportunity to the result. On acquisition: gain the Criminal Groups utility expertise.

talents.name: Shadow Step
talents.summary: After you Disengage, spend 2 focus: one Thievery test vs. each enemy's Cognitive defense (advantage while in cover or obscured); each success hides you from that enemy as if you'd hidden with Stealth.

talents.name: Fast Talker
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus to gain 2 actions usable only for spiritual skill tests via Use a Skill, Gain Advantage, or Agent-talent actions. In conversations/endeavors, the GM may let you convert both into an extra contribution.

talents.name: Trickster's Hand
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus to gain 2 actions usable only for physical skill tests via Use a Skill, Gain Advantage, or Agent-talent actions. In conversations/endeavors, the GM may let you convert both into an extra contribution.

talents.name: Rousing Presence
talents.summary: Choose an ally you can influence; they gain the Determined condition until the end of the scene.

talents.name: Steadfast Challenge
talents.summary: Spend 1 focus; test Discipline vs. Spiritual defense of an enemy you can influence. Success: they become Disoriented and take a disadvantage on tests against you until the end of their next turn.

talents.name: Withering Retort
talents.summary: Before a character you can sense resolves an attack test against you, use Steadfast Challenge as this reaction and increase your deflect against that attack by your Discipline ranks.

talents.name: Calm Appeal
talents.summary: When your Steadfast Challenge Disorients a character, spend 1 focus to also pacify them for the scene (no unprovoked hostile actions; broken if they're attacked or an ally of theirs is injured or killed). Resisting your Steadfast Challenge also costs extra focus equal to your Discipline ranks.

talents.name: Peaceful Solution
talents.summary: In combat, if every non-minion enemy is pacified and all other non-minion characters agree to stand down, combat immediately ends and becomes a conversation. Usable outside combat at GM's discretion.

talents.name: Collected
talents.summary: Cognitive and Spiritual defenses increase by 2.

talents.name: Well Dressed
talents.summary: While visibly wearing Presentable armor or fashionable clothing, gain an advantage on your first Deception, Leadership, or Persuasion test each scene. On acquisition: gain the Fashion cultural expertise.

talents.name: High Society Contacts
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus to add an Opportunity to a test made to interact socially in high society. On acquisition: gain the High Society cultural expertise.

talents.name: Practiced Oratory
talents.summary: When you use Rousing Presence or Steadfast Challenge, spend up to your Persuasion ranks in focus to affect that many additional targets.

talents.name: Customary Garb
talents.summary: While visibly wearing Presentable armor or clothing appropriate to your station, Physical and Spiritual defenses increase by 2.

talents.name: Devoted Presence
talents.summary: When you use Rousing Presence, spend 1 focus per target to remove any of these conditions from them: Prone, Slowed, Stunned, Surprised.

talents.name: Stalwart Presence
talents.summary: When you use Rousing Presence, spend 1 focus to increase one of the target's defenses (your choice) by 2 until the end of the next round.

talents.name: Sage Counsel
talents.summary: After you use the Aid reaction on an ally, spend 1 focus to grant them the benefits of your Rousing Presence (no action required).

talents.name: Galvanize
talents.summary: Once per scene, choose an ally you can influence; they roll their recovery die (no action required) and recover that much focus.

talents.name: Composed
talents.summary: Maximum and current focus increase by your tier, scaling up as your tier increases.

talents.name: Applied Motivation
talents.summary: When you cause a character to recover focus, they recover extra focus equal to half your Lore ranks (rounded up).

talents.name: Inspired Zeal
talents.summary: After an ally you can sense spends their Determined condition to add an Opportunity, choose up to your Discipline ranks in other allies you can influence; each recovers 1 focus (no action required).

talents.name: Sound Advice
talents.summary: After an ally you can influence fails a skill test, spend 1 focus to use your Rousing Presence on them.

talents.name: Lessons in Patience
talents.summary: After you use Rousing Presence, the target recovers 1 focus. On acquisition: gain the Motivational Speech utility expertise.

talents.name: Instill Confidence
talents.summary: When you use Rousing Presence, you may make the target Focused until the end of the scene instead of Determined.

talents.name: Foresight
talents.summary: At the beginning of combat and at the start of each of your turns, gain an additional reaction.

talents.name: Practical Demonstration
talents.summary: After you succeed on a test to attack or Gain Advantage, use your Rousing Presence as a free action.

talents.name: Mighty
talents.summary: On a weapon or unarmed hit, each action spent on the attack adds 1 + your tier extra damage.

talents.name: Guiding Oration
talents.summary: After you succeed on a Gain Advantage test, choose an ally you can influence within 10 feet of your target; until the end of their next turn they gain an advantage on their next test against that target.

talents.name: Rallying Shout
talents.summary: Rousing Presence can target an Unconscious ally you can sense: they wake, and if at 0 health they roll their recovery die and regain health equal to the result + your Leadership ranks.

talents.name: Seek Quarry
talents.summary: After 1 minute of mental preparation, designate a character you can sense (or have previously encountered) as your quarry until you end the effect (free action) or pick a new quarry. You gain an advantage on tests to find, attack, or study your quarry.

talents.name: Tagging Shot
talents.summary: Move up to 5 feet and make a ranged weapon attack vs. Physical defense. On a hit or graze, the target also becomes your quarry.

talents.name: Combat Training
talents.summary: Once per round, when you miss a weapon attack, you may graze one target without spending focus. On acquisition: gain one weapon expertise, one armor expertise, and the Military Life cultural expertise.

talents.name: Sharp Eye
talents.summary: After observing your quarry (2 actions) or another character for 1+ minute, test Perception vs. their Cognitive defense. Success: learn one of — their lowest attribute, their lowest defense, or whether one chosen resource (health/focus/Investiture) is below half.

talents.name: Steady Aim
talents.summary: Until the end of your turn, your ranged weapons' short and long ranges increase by half, and ranged weapon hits deal extra damage equal to your Perception ranks.

talents.name: Exploit Weakness
talents.summary: Use the Gain Advantage action as a free action, targeting only your quarry.

talents.name: Backstep
talents.summary: After making a ranged attack, spend 2 focus to Disengage as a free action; if you end your turn in cover or where the enemy's senses are obscured, you gain the Brace action's benefit.

talents.name: Unrelenting Salvo
talents.summary: You may Strike your quarry more than once per turn with the same ranged weapon, ignoring the one-Strike-per-hand limit.

talents.name: Hardy
talents.summary: Maximum and current health increase by 1 per level, retroactively and for all future levels.

talents.name: Startling Blow
talents.summary: Unarmed or improvised weapon attack vs. Cognitive defense of a target your size or smaller. On a hit or graze, they're also Surprised until the end of your next turn.

talents.name: Fatal Thrust
talents.summary: Light melee weapon attack vs. Cognitive defense of a target that is Surprised, can't sense you, or doesn't see you as a threat; two advantages if the weapon is Discreet. Add +4d4 damage (6d4 at tier 3, 8d4 at tier 4, 10d4 at tier 5); each damage die showing its maximum imposes -2 on the target's injury roll.

talents.name: Killing Edge
talents.summary: Knives and slings you wield gain the Deadly and Quickdraw expert traits. On acquisition: gain the Knives and Slings weapon expertises.

talents.name: Shadowing
talents.summary: Advantage on tests to avoid being sensed by your quarry; your quarry has disadvantage to sense you. When you succeed vs. an enemy's Spiritual defense while in cover or obscured, you may spend 3 focus to make them your quarry.

talents.name: Cold Eyes
talents.summary: After you kill or incapacitate your quarry, recover 1 focus and designate a new quarry you can sense (GM may exclude non-threats).

talents.name: Mighty
talents.summary: On a weapon or unarmed hit, each action spent on the attack adds 1 + your tier extra damage.

talents.name: Swift Strikes
talents.summary: Spend 1 focus to make a second Strike action with a hand you already used to Strike this turn.

talents.name: Sidestep
talents.summary: Gain an additional reaction at the start of combat and each of your turns, usable only to Dodge. Disabled while wearing armor with deflect 2 or higher.

talents.name: Animal Bond
talents.summary: Upgrades a reward animal companion into a partner: simple two-way communication; +1 to all your defenses while they're sensed within 10 feet; a free action on your turn grants them 1-2 actions immediately (borrowed from their next turn); they gain the Track action (2 actions: a target within their reach becomes your quarry). At 0 health they retreat instead of falling Unconscious.

talents.name: Deadly Trap
talents.summary: Conceal a 5-foot trap within reach (max active traps = Survival ranks; find/disarm DC = your Spiritual defense). Triggered: attack with Survival (advantage vs. your quarry, can't graze). Entangling (vs. Cognitive): 2d4 impact, Immobilized 1 round, area becomes difficult terrain. Impaling (vs. Physical): 2d4 keen, Afflicted [vital 3 + Survival ranks] 1 round, and you may spend 1 action to inflict an injury.

talents.name: Experienced Trapper
talents.summary: Forage while still resting normally; automatically succeed on forage tests where food/water exist, sustaining up to your Survival ranks in characters; after a wilderness long rest, craft natural tools using Survival instead of Crafting. Deadly Traps improve to 2d6 damage and 2-round conditions.

talents.name: Protective Bond
talents.summary: Assign your animal companion to guard an ally within 30 feet; while the companion stays in that ally's reach, the ally receives your Animal Bond defense bonus instead of you. End it (free action) while within 30 feet and able to influence them; otherwise ends with the scene.

talents.name: Surefooted
talents.summary: Movement rate increases by 10 feet, and damage from falling or dangerous terrain is reduced by 2 × your tier.

talents.name: Feral Connection
talents.summary: Your animal companion gains +5 × your tier max/current health (scales with tier), +2 to all three defenses, and +your Survival ranks on their tests. On acquisition: gain the Animal Care utility expertise.

talents.name: Hunter's Edge
talents.summary: Your animal companion gains an advantage on tests against your quarry. Deadly Traps improve to 2d8 damage and 3-round conditions.

talents.name: Pack Hunting
talents.summary: When an ally in your line of effect attacks your quarry, spend 1 focus to add your Survival ranks to their attack test result or their damage roll (your choice).

talents.name: Decisive Command
talents.summary: You gain a command die (starts at d4). Spend 1 focus: an ally you can influence within 20 feet may roll your command die with their next test (before the end of their next turn) and add it to one die roll of that test other than the plot die.

talents.name: Combat Coordination
talents.summary: After you Strike, use Decisive Command without spending an action; if your Strike didn't hit, it costs no focus either.

talents.name: Imposing Posture
talents.summary: After an enemy within your weapon's reach resists your influence, they become Disoriented.

talents.name: Mighty
talents.summary: On a weapon or unarmed hit, each action spent on the attack adds 1 + your tier extra damage.

talents.name: Resilient Hero
talents.summary: Before your health would drop to 0, it instead becomes equal to your Athletics modifier.

talents.name: Valiant Intervention
talents.summary: Spend 1 focus to move up to 10 feet, then test Athletics vs. an enemy's Spiritual defense; success gives them a disadvantage on tests against your allies.

talents.name: Hardy
talents.summary: Maximum and current health increase by 1 per level, retroactively and for all future levels.

talents.name: Resolute Stand
talents.summary: Your Valiant Intervention no longer provokes Reactive Strikes, and you may spend focus up to your Leadership ranks to target that many additional enemies with it.

talents.name: Demonstrative Command
talents.summary: Your command die increases one size. Spend 1 focus to add your command die to one of your own Athletics, Agility, or Leadership d20 rolls.

talents.name: Composed
talents.summary: Maximum and current focus increase by your tier, scaling up as your tier increases.

talents.name: Well Supplied
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus to add an Opportunity to a test to requisition resources. On acquisition: gain the Military Logistics utility expertise.

talents.name: Relentless March
talents.summary: Your Decisive Command also increases the target's movement by 10 feet, and they ignore the Exhausted, Slowed, and Surprised conditions.

talents.name: Synchronized Assault
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus; test Leadership vs. an enemy's Cognitive defense. Success: up to your Leadership ranks in allies each gain 1 action for an extra Strike against that target. Failure: only one ally gains the action.

talents.name: Through the Fray
talents.summary: An ally you can influence within 20 feet may Disengage or Gain Advantage as a reaction.

talents.name: Customary Garb
talents.summary: While visibly wearing Presentable armor or clothing appropriate to your station, Physical and Spiritual defenses increase by 2.

talents.name: Confident Command
talents.summary: Your command die increases one size. Spend 1 focus to add your command die to one of your own Intimidation, Leadership, or Persuasion d20 rolls.

talents.name: Authority
talents.prerequisiteOther: Title granting command of at least 5 people
talents.summary: Double the range of your Leader talents that affect allies, and double the number of allies they can affect.

talents.name: Cutthroat Tactics
talents.summary: An ally holding your command die may raise the stakes on their test instead of rolling it; if they then roll a Complication, you recover 1 focus.

talents.name: Rumormonger
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus to add an Opportunity to a test to spread misinformation or gather rumors. On acquisition: gain the Scandal utility expertise.

talents.name: Baleful
talents.summary: Characters must spend extra focus equal to your tier to resist your influence.

talents.name: Shrewd Command
talents.summary: Your command die increases one size. Spend 1 focus to add your command die to one of your own Deception, Insight, or Leadership d20 rolls.

talents.name: Tactical Ploy
talents.summary: Test Deception vs. a target's Cognitive defense to make them lose one reaction and take a disadvantage on their next cognitive or spiritual test.

talents.name: Well Dressed
talents.summary: While visibly wearing Presentable armor or fashionable clothing, gain an advantage on your first Deception, Leadership, or Persuasion test each scene. On acquisition: gain the Fashion cultural expertise.

talents.name: Set at Odds
talents.summary: Spend focus equal to the number of targets (characters you can influence); test Leadership vs. the highest Spiritual defense among them. Success: the targets become hostile toward each other.

talents.name: Grand Deception
talents.summary: Spend 3 focus; test Deception (DC 15) to reveal that an earlier situation involved a ruse of yours, retroactively changing one reasonable detail.

talents.name: Erudition
talents.summary: Temporarily gain one cultural or utility expertise you lack plus 1 bonus rank in each of two chosen non-surge cognitive skills (may exceed your normal rank cap; doesn't count for prerequisites). After a long rest with library access, you may reassign these picks.

talents.name: Efficient Engineer
talents.summary: Gain a utility expertise in Armor, Equipment, or Weapon Crafting, plus one starter fabrial (amplifying painrial, numbing painrial, or spanreed pair with emotion bracelet). Crafting/invention tests gain +2 Opportunity range, and raw material costs are halved.

talents.name: Deep Study
talents.summary: Your Erudition grants one additional cultural/utility expertise and two additional non-surge cognitive skills, reassignable the same way.

talents.name: Fine Handiwork
talents.summary: When crafting an item or inventing a fabrial, one advanced feature costs a single upgrade instead of two (once per item).

talents.name: Experimental Tinkering
talents.summary: Crafting tests gain +1 Opportunity range and crafting time is halved. You may spend a long rest reconfiguring your Prized Acquisition fabrial into a different fabrial of equal or lower tier (no new materials; the old fabrial is lost on completion; a failed spren-trapping Lore test keeps the old fabrial but locks retries until your next long rest).

talents.name: Prized Acquisition
talents.summary: Gain a specialist expertise in Fabrial Crafting and a special gem: your first fabrial crafted with it skips the usual time requirements (materials still needed), and it satisfies the gem requirement for a unique fabrial of tier equal to your character tier. It can't be sold; if lost, replace after a long rest (GM's call), and you can salvage it from a fabrial during downtime for reuse.

talents.name: Inventive Design
talents.summary: When crafting with your Prized Acquisition gem, you may select an effect 1 tier higher than the tier you're crafting.

talents.name: Overcharge
talents.summary: Once per turn, raise the stakes on an attack test made with a fabrial. Spending an Opportunity from that test lets you Strike with the fabrial as a free action (not counting against per-hand Strike limits); the GM may spend a Complication to give the fabrial a new drawback until you succeed on a DC 15 Crafting test (2 actions).

talents.name: Overwhelm with Details
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus to make a cognitive or spiritual test using your Lore modifier instead of the usual skill's modifier.

talents.name: Strategize
talents.summary: After you Gain Advantage using an Erudition-granted skill, you may grant that advantage to an ally instead; spend 2 focus to also block the target from using reactions against that ally.

talents.name: Composed
talents.summary: Maximum and current focus increase by your tier, scaling up as your tier increases.

talents.name: Deep Contemplation
talents.summary: Reassign up to 2 of the skills and expertises granted by your Erudition on the spot.

talents.name: Contingency
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus to remove a Complication from the test of an ally within 20 feet.

talents.name: Mind and Body
talents.summary: Gain a weapon expertise. Erudition grants one additional skill, and its skill picks may now include physical skills.

talents.name: Know Your Moment
talents.summary: Your defenses increase by 2 from the start of each round until you take your turn.

talents.name: Keen Insight
talents.summary: After you Gain Advantage, the target must resist your influence or take a disadvantage on their next test.

talents.name: Turning Point
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus; test Deduction vs. the Cognitive defense of the scene's top enemy leader (advantage if you took a slow turn). Success: you and your allies each gain an extra action on your next turns.

talents.name: Field Medicine
talents.summary: Spend 1 focus; test Medicine (DC 15) to treat a conscious character. Success: they add your Medicine ranks to their recovery die result.

talents.name: Anatomical Insight
talents.summary: When you hit with an unarmed attack, spend 1 focus or an Opportunity to make the target Exhausted with a penalty of half your Medicine ranks.

talents.name: Swift Healer
talents.summary: Use Field Medicine as a free action, and your healing abilities restore extra health equal to your Medicine ranks.

talents.name: Ongoing Care
talents.summary: Forgo your own rest benefits to treat an ally: test Medicine (DC 10) to remove one condition caused by their injury (usable on a given ally once per 24 hours). On acquisition: gain the Mental Health Care utility expertise.

talents.name: Emotional Intelligence
talents.summary: Gain the Diagnosis utility expertise. Erudition grants one additional skill, and its skill picks may now include spiritual skills.

talents.name: Collected
talents.summary: Cognitive and Spiritual defenses increase by 2.

talents.name: Applied Medicine
talents.summary: Your healing abilities restore extra health equal to your Lore ranks.

talents.name: Resuscitation
talents.summary: Spend 3 focus when using Field Medicine to attempt to revive an Unconscious character or one who died very recently.

talents.name: Vigilant Stance
talents.summary: Enter Vigilant Stance: while in it, the focus costs of Dodge and Reactive Strike are reduced by 1, and you may switch into other stances you know as a free action.

talents.name: Practiced Kata
talents.summary: You may use fighting stances in conversations and endeavors (entering/changing costs 1 focus and counts as your contribution that round), and unless Surprised you may start any scene already in Vigilant Stance.

talents.name: Ironstance
talents.summary: New stance. While in Ironstance: advantage on Insight tests, and when a character within your reach misses or grazes you with an attack, you may make a Reactive Strike against them.

talents.name: Surefooted
talents.summary: Movement rate increases by 10 feet, and damage from falling or dangerous terrain is reduced by 2 × your tier.

talents.name: Vinestance
talents.summary: New stance. While in Vinestance: Physical and Cognitive defenses +1; after you're hit or grazed in melee, spend a reaction to test Athletics vs. the attacker's Cognitive defense — success makes them lose 1d4 focus and lets you push them up to 5 feet × your Athletics ranks.

talents.name: Flamestance
talents.summary: New stance. While in Flamestance: advantage on Intimidation tests, and while exactly one enemy is within your reach and no allies are, gain an extra action usable to attack or Gain Advantage against that enemy.

talents.name: Signature Weapon
talents.summary: Gain a weapon expertise, then choose one weapon type you have expertise in: attacks with it gain +1 Opportunity range.

talents.name: Feinting Strike
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus: melee weapon attack vs. Cognitive defense. Hit: target loses one reaction and focus equal to your Intimidation ranks. Graze: they lose half that focus (rounded up) and keep their reaction. You may spend an Opportunity from this test to gain an action usable only to Strike or activate a stance.

talents.name: Wit's End
talents.summary: Spend 1 focus: move up to half your movement, then melee weapon attack vs. Cognitive defense of a target with 0 focus. The attack ignores deflect, deals +4d6 damage (6d6 at tier 3, scaling by tier), and can't graze.

talents.name: Shard Training
talents.prerequisiteOther: Access to a Shardblade and Shardplate for training
talents.summary: Gain the Shardplate expertise plus one of Grandbow, Shardblade, or Warhammer expertise. Your Shardplate holds 2 additional charges, and your Shardblade Strikes graze additional targets equal to your ranks in the skill used.

talents.name: Windstance
talents.summary: New stance. While in Windstance: advantage on Agility tests, and while you can reach multiple enemies, use a free action to gain an action usable to Disengage or attack.

talents.name: Shattering Blow
talents.summary: When you hit with a melee attack, spend 2 focus to deplete 1 charge from the target's armor, and each target you damage is pushed 5 feet.

talents.name: Precise Parry
talents.summary: Before being hit in melee, spend 1 focus to try turning the hit into a graze: unarmed, test Athletics vs. the triggering attack (disadvantage unless the attacker uses a Shardblade); armed, test Light or Heavy Weaponry vs. the triggering attack.

talents.name: Stonestance
talents.summary: New stance. While in Stonestance: your deflect increases by 1, and enemies within your reach must spend an extra action to attack your allies who aren't in Stonestance.

talents.name: Mighty
talents.summary: On a weapon or unarmed hit, each action spent on the attack adds 1 + your tier extra damage.

talents.name: Bloodstance
talents.summary: New stance. While in Bloodstance: your Opportunity range for attacks and physical tests expands by 2, but your Physical, Cognitive, and Spiritual defenses each decrease by 2.

talents.name: Meteoric Leap
talents.summary: Spend 2 focus to leap up to a quarter of your movement and make an unarmed attack vs. Physical defense of multiple targets, dealing double damage and knocking weaker targets Prone. Advantage on the test while wearing Shardplate.

talents.name: Cautious Advance
talents.summary: Move up to half your movement rate ignoring difficult terrain, then gain 2 actions usable only to Brace or Gain Advantage.

talents.name: Defensive Position
talents.summary: Your Brace action imposes two disadvantages on attacks against you instead of one, and allies may Brace behind your shield.

talents.name: Formation Drills
talents.summary: Allies within 10 feet who use the Brace action gain the benefits of your Defensive Position as if they had it.

talents.name: Wary
talents.summary: You can't be Surprised while you have focus, and involuntary focus loss is reduced by your Discipline ranks.

talents.name: Combat Training
talents.summary: Once per round, when you miss a weapon attack, you may graze one target without spending focus. On acquisition: gain one weapon expertise, one armor expertise, and the Military Life cultural expertise.

talents.name: Devastating Blow
talents.summary: Make a melee weapon attack vs. Physical defense, rolling an extra 2d8 damage.

talents.name: Hardy
talents.summary: Maximum and current health increase by 1 per level, retroactively and for all future levels.

talents.name: Swift Strikes
talents.summary: Spend 1 focus to make a second Strike action with a hand you already used to Strike this turn.

talents.name: First Ideal (Dustbringer)
talents.summary: Begin bonding an ashspren. Gain Investiture (maximum 2 + your higher of Awareness or Presence) and the Breathe Stormlight, Enhance, and Regenerate actions. Grants the goal 'Speak the First Ideal'; completing it makes you Empowered for the scene and grants the Abrasion and Division surge skills at 1 rank each.

talents.name: Second Ideal (Dustbringer)
talents.summary: Grants the goal 'Speak the Second Ideal'; completing it makes you Empowered for the scene and lets you use Enhance as a free action without spending Investiture.

talents.name: Third Ideal (Dustbringer)
talents.summary: Grants the goal 'Speak the Third Ideal'; completing it makes you Empowered for the scene and lets you manifest your spren as a Radiant Shardblade.

talents.name: Fourth Ideal (Dustbringer)
talents.summary: Grants the goal 'Speak the Fourth Ideal'; completing it makes you Empowered for the scene and lets you call a swarm of flamespren as Radiant Shardplate.

talents.name: Deepened Bond
talents.summary: Your spren bond range increases from 30 to 100 feet, and spending focus to give your spren a task costs 1 less (minimum 1).

talents.name: Searing Dust Storm
talents.summary: Spend Investiture while Moving to kick up an obscuring dust cloud along your path; enemies inside take extra damage equal to your Discipline ranks the first time each round.

talents.name: Invested
talents.summary: Maximum Investiture increases by your tier, scaling up as your tier increases.

talents.name: Wound Regeneration
talents.summary: When you use Regenerate, you may spend 2 Investiture to instantly recover from a temporary injury, or 3 Investiture for a permanent one (instead of or in addition to healing).

talents.name: Take Squire (Dustbringer)
talents.summary: Choose squires you've known for at least a game session who haven't bonded a Radiant spren, granting each neither, one, or both of your surges plus standard squire benefits. Maximum squires equal your current Ideal (e.g. three at the Third Ideal).
